title: Fig. 3 in Phylogenetic relationships and systematics of tapeworms of the family Davaineidae (Cestoda, Cyclophyllidea), with emphasis on species in rodents
author: Lehtonen Carlos Feliu Heikki Henttonen, Voitto Haukisalmi Alexis Ribas Jean-Pierre Hugot Serge Morand Kittipong Chaisiri Kerstin Junker Sonja Matthee Andrea Spickett Jukka T.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 3. Phylogenetic relationships between isolates of the family Davaineidae (including Inermicapsifer spp.) based on concatenated sequences of the mitochondrial NADH dehydrogenase subunit 1 gene (nad1) and the large subunit ribosomal RNA gene (28S). The topology is based on Bayesian phylogeny inference.
steps_raw: Fig. 3. Phylogenetic relationships between isolates of the family Davaineidae (including Inermicapsifer spp.) based on concatenated sequences of the mitochondrial NADH dehydrogenase subunit 1 gene (nad1) and the large subunit ribosomal RNA gene (28S). The topology is based on Bayesian phylogeny inference.
